title: Unstated identification Hess(lambda_i)=H^{(i)} across two parametrizations

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
Lemma (lem:hess) defines $H^{(i)}=\operatorname{Hess}_{(\alpha,\beta)}\gamma_i$, the Hessian of the ORDERED root map $\Psi_{\boxplus}$. Corollary (cor:psd) works with the hyperbolic eigenvalues $\lambda_i(x,a,b)$ of $f$ in direction $e_1$ and then asserts, at $x=0,a=\alpha,b=\beta$, that 'Hess$\,\lambda_i=H^{(i)}$'. This identification is correct but is never derived.

The missing computation: the roots of $t\mapsto f(x+t,a,b)$ are exactly $t=\gamma_i(a,b)-x$, so $\lambda_i(x,a,b)=\gamma_i(a,b)-x$. Hence the $x$-dependence is affine and the $(a,b)$-block of $\operatorname{Hess}\lambda_i$ equals $H^{(i)}$ at $x=0$. One must also note that the descending order of the $\lambda_i$ matches that of the $\gamma_i$, which is what makes $c_i=\gamma_i$ a correctly-ordered ($c_1\ge\dots\ge c_n$) coefficient sequence for the Abel summation. Without this explicit relation the corollary's evaluation step is unjustified.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
I reviewed Lemma~\ref{lem:hess} (definition $H^{(i)}=\operatorname{Hess}_{(\alpha,\beta)}(\Psi_\boxplus)_i$) and Corollary~\ref{cor:psd}, where the eigenvalue identification is needed.

**Finding.** The current solution already carried out the restriction argument $\operatorname{Hess}_{(x,a,b)}\lambda_i = \begin{psmallmatrix}0&0\\0&-H^{(n+1-i)}\end{psmallmatrix}$ (eq:Hesslambda-block) and the eigenvalue formula $\lambda_i(z)=x-\rho_{n+1-i}(a,b)$ (eq:lambda-eq-rho). What was missing — and is exactly the gap the critic identified — was the *explicit link between the two parametrizations*: namely that the roots $\rho_j(a,b)$ of the Walsh polynomial $x'\mapsto f(x',a,b)$ are literally the root-map coordinate functions $(\Psi_\boxplus)_j(a,b)$ whose Hessians define $H^{(j)}$, plus the explicit statement that the $x$-dependence is affine.

**Fix (written into solution.tex).** I inserted a new paragraph *"The two root parametrizations coincide"* just before the per-eigenvalue Hessian block computation, establishing:

1. $x'\mapsto f(x',a,b) = (p\boxplus_n q)(x')$ by eq:walsh + Lemma~\ref{lem:walsh-eq}, hence its decreasingly ordered roots satisfy $\rho_j(a,b)=(\Psi_\boxplus)_j(a,b)$ (new eq:rho-eq-rootmap) — the same functions whose Hessians define $H^{(j)}$.
2. Substituting into eq:lambda-eq-rho yields $\lambda_i(x,a,b)=x-(\Psi_\boxplus)_{n+1-i}(a,b)$ (new eq:lambda-eq-rootmap), the exact analogue of the gap's "$\lambda_i=\gamma_i-x$", with the sign of $x$ and the index reversal $i\mapsto n+1-i$ explicitly attributed to the eigenvalue-ordering convention of Definition~\ref{def:hyp} (eigenvalues are the decreasingly listed roots $t=x-\rho_j$ of $t\mapsto f(x-t,a,b)$).
3. From this, $\operatorname{Hess}_{(a,b)}\rho_j=\operatorname{Hess}_{(a,b)}(\Psi_\boxplus)_j=H^{(j)}$ now holds *by definition*, and the affine-in-$x$ property (coefficient of $x$ is the constant $1$) is stated explicitly, justifying the vanishing $x$-block.

The ordering-consistency point the critic raised is handled: both $\rho_j$ and $(\Psi_\boxplus)_j$ list the roots of the **same** polynomial in the **same** decreasing order $\gamma_1>\dots>\gamma_n$ (standing convention of Lemma~\ref{lem:rootreg}), and the Abel summation uses correctly-ordered weights $c_1\ge\dots\ge c_n$.

**Numerical check.** I verified for random degree-3 inputs that the ordered roots of the Walsh polynomial coincide with $\Psi_\boxplus$ and that $\lambda_i=x-\rho_{n+1-i}$ reproduces the sorted roots of $t\mapsto f(x-t,a,b)$ exactly.

**Caveat.** No LaTeX toolchain is available in the workspace, so I could not compile; I checked brace/environment balance manually (the apparent `\[`/`\]` mismatch is a false positive from `\\[2pt]` array row-spacing, not display math). The inserted block uses two balanced `equation` environments.

Considering the gap fully closed.
